[2018-03-13 03:38] apooche: Sorry I don't know the topic, just wanted to shitpost. But sometimes you get short term units first so you have enough tempo to buy long term units after. Rhino rush OP and all that.
[2018-03-13 03:39] mtanzer: no worries just thought I'd ask, nice to hear from lots of different players when possible
[2018-03-13 03:41] apooche: I thought 307ths article kind of hinted at this when he said short term units effectively get long term value by pressuring opponent and freeing up resources. I would example Tia: Tia into botches often will just lose to pure soak survival, but Tia into tarsiers won't.
[2018-03-13 03:42] mtanzer: yeah I think I agree with most of the points in the article but I get the feeling 307th and I weight the importance of some of them differently and it actually leads us to play differently in games
[2018-03-13 03:43] mtanzer: like when I'm buying early attackers (often before reaching the absorb barrier), I'm usually not thinking about whether they're short or long term
[2018-03-13 03:43] mtanzer: even if I expect a long game
[2018-03-13 03:46] apooche: People used to always use the term "permanent attack" when comparing positions, but it has fallen out of favor because it isn't really that useful of a metric.

[2018-03-13 10:26] zugz: i didn't see any good blockers in the set so i took the advice to keep my drone count under 15
[2018-03-13 10:26] zugz: and focus on making attackers
[2018-03-13 10:26] zugz: but this guy just kept making drones the whole game and i never had enough to finish him
[2018-03-13 10:26] zugz: and eventually i just ran out of steam
[2018-03-13 10:31] p0lari (Polari): they didn't just sit on their drones though
[2018-03-13 10:31] p0lari (Polari): but sold them off to steelforges
[2018-03-13 10:32] p0lari (Polari): that's a pretty interesting set
[2018-03-13 10:35] p0lari (Polari): I think your opponent had the right idea of going fairly high econ, getting green attackers and walls until you run out of walls, then selling off your drones
[2018-03-13 10:35] p0lari (Polari): though I'd guess the best line would involve an asteri somewhere and not quite that many forges
[2018-03-13 10:36] mtanzer: on t5 when you bought steelforge, I would've gotten a second conduit to make triple iso the next turn
[2018-03-13 10:37] mtanzer: keeping the drone count under 15 and focusing on buying attackers is the right idea.  so go for more iso kronus and less pixies 😃
[2018-03-13 10:38] mtanzer: I don't think asteri cannon happens that set
[2018-03-13 10:39] medar: Isos better than just tarsiers with few sentinels?
[2018-03-13 10:40] p0lari (Polari): Hmm, maybe I'll have to take a more serious look after work
[2018-03-13 10:41] mtanzer: I know syncing up isos does beat tarsiers in base+iso, but sentinel is pretty good and there was no other soak so I'm not sure.  sentinel means you have to get all 3 colors on a little higher econ but it does give so much soak, it might be good
[2018-03-13 10:41] p0lari (Polari): But I like the idea top had in that game
[2018-03-13 10:42] mtanzer: I think top got too many drones, I really don't think the set was that high econ.  but I think we were a little behind early on so he got away with it
[2018-03-13 10:42] p0lari (Polari): I mean I don't think his execution was anywhere near optimal
[2018-03-13 10:43] p0lari (Polari): But the rough plan of getting more stuff out and converting to F and steelforge clicks once you're out of walls
[2018-03-13 10:44] mtanzer: yeah steelforge clicks are really nice lategame but like buying 20+ drones and 3 steelforges is just worse than doing 1 or 2 steelforges and less drones faster
[2018-03-13 10:45] mtanzer: the game is very snowbally though, I think we just needed to buy more early attack and it would've gone differently.  I'm pretty sure we could've had at least 1 more kronus instead of a pixie with a different line
[2018-03-13 10:47] p0lari (Polari): Yeah 3 steelforges is definitely excessive
[2018-03-13 10:49] zugz: that was very useful
[2018-03-13 10:49] zugz: how often can i post replays without being an annoying spammy git 😄
[2018-03-13 10:50] mtanzer: np.  good question lol.  I'm not the right one to ask since I doubt you could reach an amount that would annoy me 😃

[2018-03-13 19:06] Deleted User (Deleted User): i'm stupid :NotLikeThis:
[2018-03-13 19:06] wiwiweb: I think campaign spoiler chat should be the place for puzzles, even if they're not technically campaign
[2018-03-13 19:07] wiwiweb: @hatsuyume search for `what qualities in a set cause you to consider going breachproof` on discord for a cool series of messages about breachproof
[2018-03-13 19:09] philipdickinson (hatsuyume): sweet, thank you! i was watching lifecoach play and he sort of mumbles "is there a breachproof strategy..." to himself when he looks over the setup each game, and i wondered how that thought process works. i'll definitely have a read

[2018-03-13 19:13] namington: You want to go double animus the turn before Lucina
[2018-03-13 19:14] namington: Yeah that sort of opening seems alright

[2018-03-14 00:50] gadget246: thoughts on arms race? (power level, optimal timing, counterplay, etc.) [[arms race]]
[2018-03-14 00:52] liadahlia (Lia): imo arms race is good
[2018-03-14 00:52] liadahlia (Lia): you can think of it like a big tarsier
[2018-03-14 00:52] liadahlia (Lia): you gain 3 attack in exchange for giving them 5 defensive tempo (4 engineers and a tarsier with build time)
[2018-03-14 00:53] liadahlia (Lia): that's almost like 3 tarsiers (3 attack in exchange for giving them 3 defensive tempo)
[2018-03-14 00:55] liadahlia (Lia): ofc it's not as simple as that though. Giving them engineers is very different than not attacking them.
[2018-03-14 00:55] liadahlia (Lia): giving them engineers gives them granularity, and also there are a few units that synergize very well with engineers (antima, cauterizer, etc)
[2018-03-14 00:56] liadahlia (Lia): and giving them engineers allow them to use those engineers immediately for defense, whereas with tarsiers not attacking you can use that to deny absorb
[2018-03-14 00:56] liadahlia (Lia): also the first arms race is very easy to absorb with a single wall
[2018-03-14 00:56] ravnabergsndot (Ravna): the gauss cannon and steelsplitter have more hp that's another nuance
[2018-03-14 00:57] liadahlia (Lia): yes, the more relevant one is the SS, which allows you to get some defensive tempo if you want to block with it and spend the 5B on something more worthwhile (like another arms race)
[2018-03-14 00:58] liadahlia (Lia): Crash once said that arms race is a weird attacker, and is good in a set where no other attackers are good

[2018-03-14 09:26] elyot: Galvani + Antima is a huge combo and you should go for it almost every time. The equilibrium antima comet timings in galvani sets are earlier than most other antima comet timings.
[2018-03-14 09:26] elyot: If your opponent has like 10 engineers doing nothing, arms race is really good because the value your opponent gets from the additional engineers is even less than usual (because it arrives even further in the future than usual)
[2018-03-14 09:28] elyot: high engineer count helps reso because the #1 thing you want when going reso is a lot of Drones, once reso is around 3-4 turns before arrival, Drones become a better investment than Tarsiers and you should waste red even. With energy being free, you can make more Drones even sooner.
[2018-03-14 09:29] elyot: Grid seems OK since Xeno is a blue sink but exact timing will depend on what your opponent does, it's rare to grid in response to antima because you're usually trying to threaten to counter-antima
[2018-03-14 09:30] elyot: frost brooder is probably crap if your opponent is getting a lot of Xenos
[2018-03-14 09:30] elyot: and it does little against resophore in the super late game
[2018-03-14 09:30] elyot: it can be OK to deny 7 soak on a low lifespan defense grid

[2018-03-15 20:54] supergreyman (Greyman): I think T4 Tia Thurnax rush feels pretty easy to defend against.
[2018-03-15 20:55] supergreyman (Greyman): Because when your opponent goes super aggressive with CA on a single turn, you have time to build walls and stuff.
[2018-03-15 20:56] f300xen (f0x): t4 tia is just a losing line every game
[2018-03-15 21:00] supergreyman (Greyman): The thing is, if you make a preemptive defense with, let's say Steelsplitter, you just need to make at least 5 defense a turn for 3 turns (because the other 2 damage will be absorbed by the Steelsplitter). Wall+Forcefield is enough to hold off Tia on its own.
[2018-03-15 21:02] f300xen (f0x): tia is also terrible against breachproof because you can't deal enough damage with it so there's that
[2018-03-15 21:03] f300xen (f0x): going first, is this set rush xae
[2018-03-15 21:05] mtanzer: looks like a comet set with ebb turbine
[2018-03-15 21:05] mtanzer: can get xae afterwards
[2018-03-15 21:05] f300xen (f0x): well my opponent got comet first guess i lose
[2018-03-15 21:06] mtanzer: sometimes getting second comet is fine.  ebb makes you want to get comet faster than usual though.  and doomed drone a little bit too but ebb is really helpful
[2018-03-15 21:06] f300xen (f0x): my opponent got reso
[2018-03-15 21:06] f300xen (f0x): i feel like that isn't right
[2018-03-15 21:06] mtanzer: reso is a good followup for comet since you have all this defense anyway
[2018-03-15 21:07] Deleted User (Deleted User): why would you "rush" xae
[2018-03-15 21:07] Deleted User (Deleted User): you get it at the normal time
[2018-03-15 21:07] mtanzer: so build-time attack means they have more time to attack into your engineers, plus the defense tempo just allows you to go for the best attacker in the set which is reso
[2018-03-15 21:08] f300xen (f0x): he got 16 engineers and i literally can't defend against that much, guess i lose
[2018-03-15 21:08] mtanzer: yeah with ebb turbine the first comet can be painful sometimes
[2018-03-15 21:08] f300xen (f0x): i guess i keep on making the mistake of not getting comet first
[2018-03-15 21:09] mtanzer: it's not so much that you have to get it first, just that you can't wait too long to set it up or their comet will be too good.   Like it's ok if they get it first if they had to do it on pretty low econ and can't build many engineers
[2018-03-15 21:10] f300xen (f0x): yeah, that makes sense
[2018-03-15 21:10] mtanzer: then you can defend it and get a good counter-comet.  but if you waited long enough for their first comet to have 16 engineers and there isn't good soak in the set (esp. non-prompt like shredder) that's a problem
[2018-03-15 21:12] f300xen (f0x): guess i'm just bad 🤷
